title: Spicy Flaky Greek Lamb Pilaf
description: A spicy and flaky Greek dish featuring lamb, cooking wine, and a blend of aromatic spices, perfect for a midnight craving. This low-calorie-friendly pilaf is a delicious and satisfying meal option.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the lamb and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the onion and garlic to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in the tomato paste, paprika, cumin, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute.
Add the cooking wine to the pot and bring to a boil. Scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven. Braise the lamb for 1 1/2 hours, or until tender.
While the lamb is cooking, thaw the phyllo dough according to the package instructions.
Remove the pot from the oven and stir in the cooked lamb. Let it cool slightly.
Layer the phyllo dough in a large bowl, brushing each layer with a little water. Spoon the lamb mixture into the center of the dough.
Fold the phyllo dough over the filling to form a flaky crust. Brush the top with a little water.
Bake the pilaf in the preheated oven for an additional 20-25 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown.
